evidence.evidence_text: ica fume: water: natural sand (0.25–1 mm): crushed stone (0.5–8 mm): coarse aggregate (4– 16 mm) = 1: 0.15: 0.22: 0.61: 0.52: 0.93. Three different steel ﬁbres with and with- out hooked-ends were added. The properties of the steel ﬁbres are given in Table 1. Two different total volume fractions of ﬁbres, 0.75 and 1.5%, were selected. Par- tial replacement of aggregate by steel ﬁbre was based on one-to-one volume basis. The concrete mixtures were designated using the following codes: REF = plain self- compacting concrete, C0.75N = concrete with 0.5% HSS + 0.25% NSH; C0.75H = con- crete with 0.5% HSS + 0.25% HSH; C1.5N = concrete with 1.0% HSS + 0.5% NSH; C1.5H = concrete with 1.0% HSS + 0.5% HSH ( Table 2 ). 2.2. Specimen preparation In mixing, cement and silica fume were blended ﬁrst in dry condition. Then, the aggregate were added and they were mixed together.
